info.title: BytePlus ModelArk - Video Generation API
info.description: Asynchronous video generation through the Seedance model family on BytePlus ModelArk.

Authorization.name: Authorization
Authorization.description: Long-term API Key issued from the ModelArk console. Pass the full header value including the Bearer prefix, e.g. 'Bearer <ARK_API_KEY>'.

text.description: Text prompt describing the desired video. English supported by all models; Seedance 2.0 / 2.0 Fast also accept Japanese, Indonesian, Spanish, and Portuguese. Recommended length under 1000 words.

url.description: Public image URL, data: base64 string (data:image/<fmt>;base64,...), or asset:// URI.

role.description: Position or purpose of the image. Required for first-and-last-frame and multimodal-reference scenarios.

url.description: Public video URL or asset:// URI. Only Seedance 2.0 series accepts video input.

role.description: Currently the only supported role is reference_video.

url.description: Public audio URL, data: base64 string (data:audio/<fmt>;base64,...), or asset:// URI. Audio cannot be input alone - at least one image or video must accompany it.

role.description: Currently the only supported role is reference_audio.

id.description: Draft task ID. ModelArk reuses the original draft inputs (model, content.text, content.image_url, generate_audio, seed, ratio, duration, camera_fixed) when promoting to a final video. Seedance 1.5 Pro only.

model.description: Model ID or endpoint ID. Examples: seedance-2-0-260128, seedance-2-0-fast-260128, seedance-1-5-pro-251215, seedance-1-0-pro-250528, seedance-1-0-pro-fast-251015, seedance-1-0-lite-t2v-250428, seedance-1-0-lite-i2v-250428.

content.description: Reference content for video generation. See ContentItem for accepted shapes (text, image_url, video_url, audio_url, draft_task).

callback_url.description: POST callback URL invoked by ModelArk on every status change. Payload mirrors the retrieve-task response.

return_last_frame.description: Return the last frame of the generated video as a PNG via last_frame_url on retrieve.

service_tier.description: default = online inference (lower RPM, lower latency). flex = offline inference (higher TPD, ~50% price). Seedance 2.0 series does not support flex.

execution_expires_after.description: Task auto-expiry threshold in seconds, calculated from created_at.

generate_audio.description: Whether the model should produce a video with synchronized audio. Only Seedance 2.0 series and Seedance 1.5 Pro honour this flag. All audio output is mono.

draft.description: Enable draft mode (Seedance 1.5 Pro only). Generates a 480p preview at lower cost. Disables last-frame return and offline inference.

safety_identifier.description: Stable, non-PII end-user identifier. Recommended to be a hash of the username/user-id/email.

resolution.description: Output resolution. Default 720p for Seedance 2.0 series, 1.5 Pro, 1.0 Lite. Default 1080p for 1.0 Pro and Pro Fast. 1080p is not supported by Seedance 1.0 Lite (reference-to-image) or Seedance 2.0 Fast.

ratio.description: Aspect ratio. adaptive lets the model pick based on inputs (Seedance 2.0 series and 1.5 Pro; image-to-video for other models).

duration.description: Generated video length in seconds. Range varies by model. Set to -1 (Seedance 2.0 series and 1.5 Pro) to let the model pick. duration is mutually exclusive with frames; if both supplied, frames wins.

frames.description: Frame count. Must satisfy 25 + 4n. Use frames for fractional-second durations. Not supported by Seedance 2.0 series or 1.5 Pro.

seed.description: Seed integer for randomness. -1 = engine-picked random seed. Same seed => similar results, not guaranteed identical.

camera_fixed.description: Append fixed-camera instruction to the prompt. Not supported in reference-to-image scenarios or by Seedance 2.0 series.

watermark.description: Whether to embed a watermark in the output.

id.description: Task ID. Stored for 7 days from created_at. Use it with GET /contents/generations/tasks/{id} to poll status.

code.description: Error code (see ModelArk error code reference).

completion_tokens.description: Tokens consumed for the video output.

total_tokens.description: Total tokens for the request. For video models input_tokens is always 0, so total_tokens == completion_tokens.

video_url.description: Generated video URL. Cleared 24 hours after completion - persist promptly.

last_frame_url.description: PNG of the last frame. Returned only when return_last_frame: true was set on create. Cleared after 24 hours.

status.description: Lifecycle state. cancelled is reachable only from queued; running tasks cannot be cancelled.

updated_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) of last status update.

duration.description: Returned when frames was not specified on create.

frames.description: Returned when frames was specified on create.

draft_task_id.description: Returned when promoting a draft to a final video.

total.description: Number of tasks matching the filter. Note: only the last 7 days of history are queryable (UTC, [T-7d, T)).

post.description: Asynchronously create a Seedance video generation task. Returns a task id; poll GET /contents/generations/tasks/{id} for status and the output video_url when status is succeeded.

get.description: List the caller's recent video generation tasks. Only the last 7 days of history are queryable (UTC, [T-7d, T)).

parameters.name: filter.task_ids
parameters.description: Filter by one or more task IDs. Repeat the parameter for each ID (e.g. filter.task_ids=id1&filter.task_ids=id2).

parameters.name: filter.model
parameters.description: Exact-match filter on the inference endpoint ID (different from the model field returned in items).

parameters.name: filter.service_tier
parameters.description: Filter by service tier (default = online inference, flex = offline inference).

get.description: Fetch the current status and (when succeeded) output URLs of a task.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Task ID returned by the create endpoint.

delete.summary: Cancel or delete a video generation task
delete.description: Behaviour by current state: queued => cancel (status -> cancelled); running => not allowed; succeeded / failed / expired => delete record (no longer queryable); cancelled => not allowed.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Task ID to cancel or delete.

204.description: Task cancelled or record deleted. No response body.
